commands.review_summary:
The PR threads the active session id into diagnostic transcript lookup for app-agent session keys and adds focused logging regression tests.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +76. Total +84 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main returns empty diagnostic context for non-cron agent keys and does not pass activeSessionId into the resolver. The PR proof also reproduces the partial after-fix path with real JSONL transcript output.
commands.review_followup: - Maintainer review should decide whether to accept a partial diagnostic enrichment after narrowing the closing claim, or require the PR to address the remaining classifier/recovery behavior.

target.title: fix(logging): resolve app-agent diagnostic transcripts
target.body:
Fixes #91505

## Summary
- Resolves stale-session diagnostic transcript context for app-agent/OAuth sessions that use ordinary `agent:<agentId>:<session>` keys instead of cron run keys.
- Threads the active diagnostic `sessionId` into the attention-log context resolver so the diagnostic can read `agents/<agentId>/sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl`.
- Keeps cron diagnostic fields unchanged while adding regression coverage for app-agent transcript lookup and attention log output.

## Root Cause
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause fix for diagnostic transcript resolution.
- **Root cause:** The source of the bug was the diagnostic context resolver treating cron run session keys as the only resolvable transcript shape. App-agent/OAuth sessions already have an agent-scoped key such as `agent:oauth-agent:main`, but the resolver did not recover the `agentId` from that key and `logSessionAttention()` did not pass the active `sessionId`, so the diagnostic had no canonical path to the persisted JSONL transcript.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The persisted transcript invariant is `stateDir/agents/<agentId>/sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl`. Reusing the shared `parseAgentSessionKey()` parser fixes the session-key source of truth, and passing the active diagnostic session id fixes the missing run/session identifier at the caller. That resolves the existing transcript path directly instead of masking the symptom with a synthetic last message or a downstream classification tweak.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High: the patch is focused on the diagnostic resolver/caller boundary, includes regression tests for direct resolver output and attention-log output, and is backed by local real-behavior proof against production modules.

## What changed
- `src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.ts` now reuses the shared agent session-key parser to recover the agent id for non-cron agent-scoped keys.
- `src/logging/diagnostic.ts` now passes the active diagnostic session id into the context resolver.
- `src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts` covers direct app-agent transcript resolution.
- `src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts` covers stale-session attention logs including the app-agent transcript `lastAssistant` field.

## What did NOT change
- **What did NOT change:** This patch does not change cron session key routing, cron diagnostic fields, session recovery classification, queue handling, transcript persistence, or any channel/provider delivery behavior. Out of scope: live OAuth login, remote channel proof, and changing the stale-session classifier itself.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth contract surface remains the existing persisted local transcript path, `stateDir/agents/<agentId>/sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl`. This is a minimal canonical diagnostic lookup change, not a schema/config/protocol migration and not a new public API. Related open PR / same-contract scan found no linked open PR for issue #91505 in the daily-fix evidence artifacts, so this does not compete with another canonical PR.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Stale-session diagnostics for app-agent/OAuth sessions can resolve the local session transcript and include the real latest assistant message instead of producing empty transcript context.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Without this context, support diagnostics classify a stale app-agent session as if it never started even when the transcript shows assistant work; maintainers and users lose the actual last assistant message needed to distinguish no-op sessions from blocked work.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw TypeScript production modules in the issue worktree on Linux, using `node --import tsx` and a real JSONL transcript under an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```text
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-91505
node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-91505-evidence/app-agent-session-diagnostic-proof.mjs
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.ts src/logging/diagnostic.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --deny-warnings src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.ts src/logging/diagnostic.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
Behavior addressed: stale-session diagnostics resolve app-agent/OAuth session transcripts.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw TypeScript production modules under node --import tsx with a real JSONL state transcript.
Exact path exercised: resolveCronSessionDiagnosticContext plus logSessionAttention from src/logging/diagnostic.ts.
session_key=agent:oauth-agent:main
session_id=oauth-session-1
session_file=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-91505-evidence/app-agent-session-diagnostic-state/agents/oauth-agent/sessions/oauth-session-1.jsonl
resolver_fields=lastAssistant="OAuth agent listed 3 pending approvals."
attention_log=stuck session: sessionId=oauth-session-1 sessionKey=agent:oauth-agent:main state=processing age=61s queueDepth=0 reason=stale_session_state classification=stale_session_state lastAssistant="OAuth agent listed 3 pending approvals." recovery=checking
ASSERT_PASS: app-agent stale-session diagnostics include lastAssistant from the real session transcript and do not require cron/run session keys
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The app-agent stale-session attention log includes `lastAssistant="OAuth agent listed 3 pending approvals."` for `sessionKey=agent:oauth-agent:main` and `sessionId=oauth-session-1`, proving the diagnostic follows the ordinary app-agent transcript path without requiring cron run key fields.
- **What was not tested:** No live OAuth provider account or remote channel was contacted; this fix is limited to local diagnostic transcript resolution and attention-log formatting. No Windows/macOS app-agent runtime was tested.

## Regression Test Plan
- **Target test file:** `src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts` and `src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** A real JSONL transcript exists at `agents/oauth-agent/sessions/oauth-session-1.jsonl`, the diagnostic key is the non-cron app-agent key `agent:oauth-agent:main`, and stale-session attention logs must include the latest assistant text.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The resolver test guards the direct agentId/sessionId transcript lookup, while the diagnostic logger test guards the production caller path that passes `sessionId` into the resolver. Together they cover the issue without adding a live OAuth/channel dependency.

```text
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 14.45s
src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts: 1 file passed, 5 tests passed

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 13.73s
src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts: 1 file passed, 69 tests passed

Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
```

## Merge risk
- **Risk labels considered:** session-state, security-boundary, availability, compatibility.
- **Risk explanation:** The merge-risk signal is limited to diagnostic session context: the patch reads an already persisted transcript path and imports the shared parser for agent-scoped session keys. It does not read credentials, tokens, private provider data beyond the existing local transcript file, does not mutate session state, and does not affect recovery decisions or message delivery.
- **Why acceptable:** Missing files still produce no `lastAssistant`, preserving best-effort diagnostic behavior. Cron behavior is unchanged, and the app-agent path only activates when both an agent-scoped session key and active session id identify the existing local transcript.

## Patch quality notes
- **Patch quality notes:** No unrelated refactor, dependency change, lockfile change, public API change, schema change, config change, protocol change, or channel/provider behavior change. Patch-quality warning signals are explained above: this is source-of-truth diagnostic lookup, not post-processing of model-consumed state.
